purpose: Add a focused Test-Driven Development blueprint to the Ananta standard blueprint catalog without creating a new orchestration path. The blueprint should reuse Hub planning, Task/Artifact flow, Policy/Approval, native worker patch/test execution and Release-Gate verification.
non_goals: Do not add client-side TDD orchestration.
Do not let the TDD blueprint bypass worker policy, approval, artifacts or trace metadata.
Do not create a second planner or worker path for TDD.
Do not claim support for every language/test framework without smoke evidence.
success_criteria: TDD appears as a standard blueprint in the blueprint catalog.
Blueprint expresses Red -> Green -> Refactor -> Verify as normal Ananta tasks/artifacts.
Generated tasks require the test to fail before implementation when feasible.
Worker/Release-Gate evidence proves at least one tiny repo TDD flow.
CLI/TUI/Web can display the same blueprint output without duplicating orchestration.

tasks.title: TDD blueprint definition add
acceptance_criteria: Standard blueprint catalog includes TDD or Test-Driven Development.
Intended use describes behavior-first code changes where tests are written before implementation.
Safety/review stance is verification required and approval-gated for patch apply.
Default outputs include TestPlanArtifact, RedTestResultArtifact, PatchPlanArtifact, GreenTestResultArtifact and RefactorChecklist.

tasks.title: TDD work profile hints add
acceptance_criteria: Blueprint exposes recommended goal modes for coding, repair and refactor tasks.
Blueprint exposes playbook hints for Red -> Green -> Refactor -> Verify.
Blueprint exposes capability hints for worker.patch.propose, worker.test.run, worker.verify.result and approval-gated apply.
Blueprint does not require a special client implementation.

tasks.title: TDD blueprint example inputs add
acceptance_criteria: Docs include at least one tiny feature example and one bugfix example.
Expected result sketch shows red test, minimal implementation, green verification and optional refactor.
Docs clearly state that TDD support depends on available project test runner and worker permissions.

tasks.title: TDD task decomposition rules define
acceptance_criteria: Defines task sequence: clarify behavior, add/adjust test, run test expecting failure, implement minimal patch, run tests expecting success, optional refactor, final verification.
Red test failure is represented as expected evidence rather than task failure when in red phase.
If project cannot run tests, blueprint degrades honestly to TestPlanArtifact and implementation is blocked or marked degraded.
Tests use deterministic planner/model fixtures.

tasks.title: TDD artifact types align with existing Artifact system
acceptance_criteria: Schemas capture behavior statement, test files, red result, patch refs, green result, refactor notes and verification refs.
Schemas reference existing PatchArtifact/TestResultArtifact/VerificationArtifact instead of duplicating them.
Artifact status distinguishes red_expected, red_missing, green_passed, green_failed, refactor_skipped and degraded.
Schema tests validate minimal and full TDD cycle examples.

tasks.title: TDD policy and approval mapping add
acceptance_criteria: Test creation/modification is treated as write activity and follows normal patch approval rules.
Patch apply remains approval-gated when policy requires it.
Running tests uses worker.test.run capabilities and command policy.
Blueprint cannot relax Hub policy or execute commands directly.

tasks.title: Tiny repo TDD smoke test add
acceptance_criteria: Smoke flow creates a TDD blueprint instance for a tiny repo feature.
Flow records red test evidence before implementation patch evidence.
Flow records green test evidence after implementation.
Smoke uses mocked model/provider output by default.
No direct main-tree mutation happens before approved apply.

tasks.title: TDD docs add
acceptance_criteria: Docs explain how users start a TDD goal.
Docs explain red/green/refactor evidence and what happens when tests cannot run.
Docs include CLI/TUI/Web visibility expectations without client-specific orchestration.
Docs state limitations honestly.

tasks.title: TDD release gate hook add
acceptance_criteria: Release gate can include the TDD tiny repo smoke when TDD blueprint runtime is claimed.
Gate fails if green phase is claimed without passing test evidence.
Gate fails if red phase is skipped without degraded explanation.
Gate reports TDD evidence artifact paths.

execution_order_summary: Define task decomposition and artifact mapping next.
Add policy mapping to ensure TDD cannot bypass Hub rules.
Add tiny repo smoke and docs after native worker patch/test flow exists.
summary_notes: This is intentionally small: TDD is mainly a blueprint/playbook plus artifact/test evidence, not a new agent architecture.
The runtime-heavy parts depend on the native worker patch/test flow.
TDD should become a strong dogfooding pattern for Ananta itself once the native worker is stable.
